metadata.archetype_justification: This system is a distributed storage caching layer whose primary operations are read-heavy data access (file reads, block reads, metadata lookups) served via gRPC and REST APIs. It has an embedded metadata store (RocksDB) but its core role is as a data access gateway between compute frameworks and underlying storage systems.
metadata.context: Data orchestration / virtual distributed file system. JVM, distributed storage caching layer.

findings.title: Managed Compute: Mix of Kubernetes and Legacy VM Support
findings.description: Alluxio has comprehensive Kubernetes deployment support (Helm charts, K8s Operator, CSI driver) but also maintains legacy VM deployment via Vagrant/Ansible. Container orchestration is not targeting a specific managed service (EKS/ECS).
findings.gap: Helm chart does not target EKS specifically. Vagrant/Ansible VM deployment path still maintained. No Fargate or serverless options.
findings.recommendation: Standardize on EKS as primary deployment target. Add EKS-specific configs (IRSA, EBS CSI, ALB Ingress). Deprecate Vagrant/VM path.

findings.title: Managed Databases: Self-Managed Embedded RocksDB
findings.description: All persistent state uses embedded RocksDB within the Master process. ZooKeeper used for leader election. No managed database services.
findings.gap: Database is entirely self-managed (embedded RocksDB). No automated failover, no managed backups, no scaling. ZooKeeper may also be self-managed.
findings.recommendation: Evaluate Aurora or DynamoDB for journal durability. Migrate ZooKeeper to managed alternative. RocksDB for hot metadata may remain architecturally appropriate.

findings.title: Network Security: No Network Isolation Defined
findings.description: No VPC, security groups, NACLs, or NetworkPolicy resources defined in any IaC. OTEL collector uses insecure communication. No TLS between components.
findings.gap: Complete absence of network security infrastructure. Services have no network isolation. No TLS configured between Master/Worker/Proxy.
findings.recommendation: Add Kubernetes NetworkPolicy to Helm chart. Deploy on EKS in private subnets. Enable gRPC TLS. Add VPC endpoints for S3 access.

findings.title: API Entry Point: No Managed Gateway
findings.description: Kubernetes Services provide internal load balancing. S3 proxy exposed without gateway-level protection. No API Gateway, ALB, or throttling.
findings.gap: No managed API entry point for REST APIs. S3 proxy directly exposed without gateway protection.
findings.recommendation: Add API Gateway in front of S3 REST proxy. Configure ALB Ingress Controller on EKS for WebUI and admin APIs.

findings.title: Auto-Scaling: No Scaling Configured
findings.description: No HPA, VPA, or Cluster Autoscaler. Master StatefulSet has fixed replicas. Worker DaemonSet is node-count-bound. All capacity statically provisioned.
findings.gap: No auto-scaling. Worker count fixed to node count. No scaling based on cache pressure or request rate.
findings.recommendation: Implement HPA for Proxy. Configure Karpenter on EKS for node scaling. Add KEDA with CloudWatch metrics for custom scaling.

findings.title: Backup and Recovery: No Backup Configuration
findings.description: No automated backup for RocksDB metadata or journal. PVCs have no snapshot lifecycle. No restore procedure documented in deployment configs.
findings.gap: No backup automation. Master PVC loss = total metadata loss. No PITR. No documented restore procedure.
findings.recommendation: Implement EBS snapshot lifecycle for Master PVCs. Configure UFS journal to S3 with versioning. Add backup CronJob. Document restore procedures.

findings.title: High Availability: Multi-Master Supported but Not Multi-AZ
findings.description: Raft consensus for Master HA supported. However, no AZ topology constraints, no pod anti-affinity, no explicit multi-AZ distribution in Helm chart.
findings.gap: HA supported architecturally but not configured for AZ fault isolation. Single AZ failure could take down all Masters.
findings.recommendation: Add topologySpreadConstraints for Master across AZs. Configure pod anti-affinity. Use WaitForFirstConsumer StorageClass.

findings.title: IaC Coverage: Partial - K8s Layer Only
findings.description: Helm charts and K8s operator cover container orchestration. No Terraform/CloudFormation/CDK for underlying cloud infrastructure (VPC, IAM, S3, EKS cluster).
findings.gap: 0% cloud infrastructure in IaC. VPC, IAM, S3, EKS cluster provisioning presumably manual.
findings.recommendation: Create Terraform or CDK modules for EKS cluster, VPC, IAM (IRSA), S3 buckets, CloudWatch. Target 90%+ coverage.

findings.title: CI/CD Automation: CI Only, No Deployment
findings.description: GitHub Actions provides CI (unit tests, integration tests, checkstyle). No deployment automation. No artifact publishing. No infrastructure pipeline.
findings.gap: Build/test automated but deployment is entirely manual. No CD pipeline for environments.
findings.recommendation: Extend GitHub Actions with container build/push to ECR, Helm chart publishing, ArgoCD deployment trigger, and IaC pipeline.

findings.title: Programming Languages: Java 8 with Compound Legacy Dependencies
findings.description: Java 8 (source/target), AWS SDK v1 (1.11.815) + v2 (2.16.104), Jetty 9.4.46 (EOL), Jersey 2.34 (pre-Jakarta), Go 1.18 (EOL), Node.js 10.11.0 (EOL).
findings.gap: Compound legacy: Java 8 + Jetty EOL + Jersey pre-Jakarta + AWS SDK v1 + PowerMock (deprecated). All three axes (language, framework, SDK) regressed.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to Java 17+ with Jakarta EE. Complete AWS SDK v1→v2. Upgrade Go to 1.22+. Upgrade Node.js to 20+.

findings.title: Monolith vs Microservices: Distributed Monolith
findings.description: Single codebase with identifiable modules (core, proxy, table, underfs) compiled into shared assemblies. Master/Worker share dependencies and versioning. S3 proxy and table catalog are logically separable.
findings.gap: Monolith with identifiable modules but shared assemblies and tight compile-time dependencies. Cannot independently version or deploy services.
findings.recommendation: Conditional/Adaptive approach: keep Master/Worker topology, extract S3 proxy and table catalog as independent services using existing gRPC contracts.

findings.title: API Versioning: Basic /api/v1 Only
findings.description: REST APIs use /api/v1 path prefix. gRPC services use integer version constants. Only one version exists with no migration path or backward compatibility guarantees.
findings.gap: Basic versioning exists but is ad-hoc. No v2 migration path. gRPC uses informal integer versioning rather than proto package versioning.
findings.recommendation: Adopt proto package versioning for gRPC. Document versioning policy. Plan /api/v2 endpoints.

findings.title: Service Discovery: K8s DNS + Static Files
findings.description: Kubernetes DNS provides service discovery in K8s deployments. ZooKeeper provides Master discovery for HA. Non-K8s uses static conf/masters and conf/workers files.
findings.gap: Partial - K8s DNS works but non-K8s path relies on static host files. No dynamic discovery for VM deployments.
findings.recommendation: For EKS, current approach is appropriate. Add AWS Cloud Map for multi-cluster scenarios. Deprecate static host files.

findings.title: Unstructured Data: Operational Data on Local/PVC Storage
findings.description: Operational data (logs, journal snapshots) stored on PersistentVolumes rather than S3. The system provides S3 access to clients but doesn't use S3 for its own data.
findings.gap: Operational data not in managed object storage. No parsing pipeline for operational data.
findings.recommendation: Configure journal snapshots and logs to S3. Use S3 lifecycle policies for retention. Enable search/analytics on operational data.

findings.title: Database Engine Version: Implicit RocksDB Version
findings.description: RocksDB version managed through Maven dependency resolution without explicit pinning or EOL tracking. ZooKeeper version similarly implicit.
findings.gap: Engine versions not explicitly pinned. No EOL tracking. No documented version-update procedure.
findings.recommendation: Pin RocksDB and ZooKeeper versions explicitly in POM. Document EOL dates and update procedures.

findings.title: Audit Logging: Not Present
findings.description: No CloudTrail or equivalent audit logging. No immutable log storage. Application logging exists (Log4j) but no security audit trail.
findings.gap: No audit logging infrastructure. No forensic capability after security incidents.
findings.recommendation: Enable CloudTrail. Configure S3 Object Lock for immutable logs. Add application-level audit logging for filesystem operations.

findings.title: Encryption at Rest: Not Present
findings.description: No encryption-at-rest for RocksDB metadata, journal data, or cached data. No KMS integration. PVCs do not reference encrypted StorageClasses.
findings.gap: All data at rest unencrypted. No KMS. No encrypted storage class.
findings.recommendation: Configure encrypted EBS StorageClasses. Enable S3 encryption for UFS. Add KMS key management via IaC.

findings.title: API Authentication: Custom SASL, No OAuth2/JWT
findings.description: Custom SASL-based authentication for gRPC. S3 signature validation for proxy. REST admin APIs and WebUI lack authentication. Auth defaults to SIMPLE mode.
findings.gap: Not token-based (no OAuth2/JWT). Admin REST APIs unauthenticated. Default auth provides no security guarantee.
findings.recommendation: Add OAuth2/JWT via API Gateway authorizers. Integrate with Cognito. Maintain S3 signature validation. Upgrade SASL to Kerberos or mTLS.

findings.title: Centralized Identity: Standalone Auth Only
findings.description: Custom JAAS login module. Identity from OS-level user. No Cognito, Okta, LDAP, OIDC, or SAML integration. No SSO.
findings.gap: Completely standalone authentication. No external IdP. No centralized identity governance.
findings.recommendation: Integrate with Cognito for admin/API access. Implement OIDC federation. Enable SSO for WebUI. Use IRSA on EKS.

findings.title: Secrets Management: Environment Variables, No Rotation
findings.description: No Secrets Manager or Vault. AWS credentials in env vars. Vagrant script writes credentials to plaintext file. Helm chart secrets mount commented out.
findings.gap: No secrets management. Credentials in env vars without rotation. Development script writes plaintext credentials.
findings.recommendation: Integrate Secrets Manager. Use IRSA on EKS to eliminate static credentials. Configure rotation. Remove plaintext credential patterns.

findings.title: Compute Hardening: No Patching or Scanning
findings.description: No patching strategy. CentOS 7 (EOL) base image. Custom compiled libfuse. No vulnerability scanning. Go 1.18 (EOL). No hardened base images.
findings.gap: No patching automation. EOL base images. Custom binaries. No vulnerability scanning. No hardened images.
findings.recommendation: Use Amazon Linux 2023 or Bottlerocket. Enable ECR scanning. Integrate Trivy in CI. Upgrade Go to 1.22+. Remove CentOS 7.

findings.title: Security Pipeline: No Security Scanning
findings.description: No Dependabot, Snyk, SonarQube, CodeQL, or container scanning. No security gates. 71 Maven POMs with hundreds of transitive dependencies go unscanned.
findings.gap: Complete absence of security scanning in pipeline. No dependency vulnerability detection. No SAST.
findings.recommendation: Add Dependabot for Maven/Go/npm. Integrate CodeQL for SAST. Add ECR scanning. Add OWASP dependency-check. Configure security gates.

findings.title: Distributed Tracing: Infrastructure Only, No App Instrumentation
findings.description: OTEL collector and agents configured for trace collection. However, no OpenTelemetry SDK imports in Java source. Tracing is agent-based auto-instrumentation only.
findings.gap: Tracing infrastructure exists but no application-level instrumentation. No custom spans. No cross-service trace propagation in code.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenTelemetry SDK with custom spans for key operations. Ensure trace propagation across Master-Worker gRPC. Export to X-Ray.

findings.title: SLO Definitions: Not Present
findings.description: No SLO/SLI definitions. No error budgets. Prometheus/Grafana deployed without SLO dashboards or alerting rules.
findings.gap: No SLOs. Cannot measure service quality or prioritize improvements.
findings.recommendation: Define SLOs for cache hit ratio (>95%), metadata p99 (<10ms), read throughput (>1GB/s). Implement error budget tracking.

findings.title: Business Metrics: Infrastructure Metrics Only
findings.description: Extensive infrastructure metrics (JVM, RPC counts, cache sizes) via Prometheus. No business outcome metrics (workload patterns, cost-per-read, cache efficiency trends).
findings.gap: Only infrastructure metrics. No business-level metrics for informed decision-making.
findings.recommendation: Add CloudWatch metrics for cache hit ratio by workload, storage tier usage, cost-per-operation. Create combined dashboards.

findings.title: Anomaly Detection: No Alerting Configured
findings.description: No alerting rules in Prometheus. No CloudWatch alarms. No anomaly detection. No PagerDuty/OpsGenie integration.
findings.gap: No alerting whatsoever. Failures undetected until user reports.
findings.recommendation: Configure AlertManager with rules for Master unreachable, Worker disconnected, RPC error rate. Enable CloudWatch anomaly detection.

findings.title: Deployment Strategy: No Staged Rollout
findings.description: No canary, blue/green, or progressive delivery. Logserver uses Recreate strategy. No ArgoCD/Flux. Manual Helm upgrades only.
findings.gap: Direct-to-production with no staged rollout. No automated deployment. No rollback automation.
findings.recommendation: Implement ArgoCD for GitOps on EKS. Configure Argo Rollouts for canary on Proxy. Rolling updates with readiness gates for stateful components.

findings.title: Integration Testing: Functional Only, No Deployment Tests
findings.description: Integration tests exist in GitHub Actions for functional correctness (minicluster). No deployment-level tests, no API contract tests, no performance regression tests.
findings.gap: No tests verify Helm upgrades succeed. No API contract tests. No performance baselines.
findings.recommendation: Add Helm install/upgrade smoke tests. API contract tests against deployed proxy. Performance baselines in CI.

findings.title: Incident Response: No Runbooks or Automation
findings.description: No runbooks. No SSM Automation. No self-healing beyond K8s pod restarts. No incident response documentation.
findings.gap: Entirely ad hoc incident response. No documented procedures for common failures.
findings.recommendation: Create runbooks for Master failover, Worker recovery, journal restore, cache invalidation. Implement SSM Automation documents.

findings.title: Observability Ownership: No Ownership Defined
findings.description: No CODEOWNERS for monitoring. No team attribution on alarms or dashboards. Generic Prometheus/Grafana without organizational structure.
findings.gap: No observability ownership. No on-call mapping. Monitoring undifferentiated.
findings.recommendation: Define per-component ownership. Create per-component dashboards with owners. Add CODEOWNERS for monitoring configs.

findings.title: Resource Tagging: No Tagging Governance
findings.description: Empty defaults for all labels and annotations in Helm chart. No cost allocation tags. No environment labels. No ownership tags.
findings.gap: No tags for cost allocation, ownership, or environment. Cannot track costs or identify ownership.
findings.recommendation: Define tagging standard. Apply default labels in Helm chart. Use default_tags in Terraform when added. Enforce via Tag Policies.

top_gaps.title: Audit Logging
top_gaps.gap: No CloudTrail or audit logging configured
top_gaps.impact: No forensic capability for security incidents

top_gaps.title: Encryption at Rest
top_gaps.gap: No encryption-at-rest for metadata or cached data

top_gaps.title: SLO Definitions
top_gaps.gap: No SLO/SLI definitions or error budgets

top_gaps.title: Secrets Management
top_gaps.gap: No secrets management; credentials in env vars

top_gaps.title: Network Security
top_gaps.gap: No network isolation in any IaC

quick_agent_wins.name: DevOps Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: CI/CD pipeline exists (INF-Q11=2, GitHub Actions with unit/integration tests)
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: Agent triggers builds, checks test status, manages PR workflows via GitHub Actions API
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Deployment automation needs to be added before full release lifecycle management

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: 177 markdown documentation files, extensive docs/ directory with Jekyll site
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: RAG-based agent indexes Alluxio documentation for developer/operator questions
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Documentation needs vector store indexing; some docs may be outdated

decomposition_strategy.rationale: Keep Master/Worker topology (architecturally sound for distributed cache). Extract S3 proxy and table catalog as independent services.

pathways.name: Move to Cloud Native
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: APP-Q2=2 (distributed monolith with identifiable but coupled modules)

triggering_questions.note: Distributed monolith with shared assemblies and tight compile-time coupling

immediate_actions: Extract S3 proxy as independent service
Define service boundaries via proto contracts

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1=3, container definitions already exist (12 Dockerfiles, Helm charts, K8s operator, CSI driver)

triggering_questions.note: INF-Q1=3 meets threshold; containerization already adopted

pathways.not_triggered_reason: Workload already has extensive containerization: 12 Dockerfiles, Helm charts, Kubernetes operator with CRDs, CSI driver.

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4=4 (no stored procedures), no commercial DB engines detected. System uses RocksDB (open source).

triggering_questions.note: No stored procedures or proprietary SQL — already open source stack

pathways.not_triggered_reason: All database technology is already open source (RocksDB, ZooKeeper). No commercial database engines detected.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q2=2 triggers technically, but embedded RocksDB is architecturally deliberate for a storage caching system requiring microsecond metadata lookups.

triggering_questions.note: Self-managed but architecturally deliberate — embedded RocksDB for latency-sensitive metadata

pathways.not_triggered_reason: Embedded RocksDB is architecturally correct for this system. Migrating metadata to a network-attached managed database would degrade the core latency characteristics that define the product.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q4=4 (sync is correct for data-gateway archetype). No data processing/ETL workloads detected in the system.

triggering_questions.note: Score 4 — synchronous is correct design for data-gateway archetype

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No data processing, ETL, or analytics workloads detected. System is a data access layer, not a data processing system.

pathways.name: Move to Modern DevOps
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q10=2 (partial IaC), INF-Q11=2 (no CD), OPS-Q5=1 (no deployment strategy), OPS-Q6=2 (functional tests only)

triggering_questions.note: Only K8s layer in IaC; 0% cloud infrastructure
No canary/blue-green; manual Helm upgrades only
Functional integration tests only; no deployment validation

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context.
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. Context describes 'Data orchestration / virtual distributed file system' with no AI-related signal terms.
